title: alp-sdk curated third-party library manifest (v1)
description: Per-library manifest for the ADR 0018 third-party library layer.  One file per library under metadata/libraries/<name>.yaml -- the single source of truth for how a curated library is wired per OS, what it needs from the target, its curation tier, its pinned upstream version and its SPDX licence.  The orchestrator emits the `integration:` wiring through the ADR 0014 --emit contract when a project declares `libraries: [<name>, ...]`; `scripts/validate_metadata.py` schema-validates every manifest, cross-checks each `requires.capabilities` key against the SoC capability vocabulary, and rejects any licence outside the allowlist.  ADR 0017 applies verbatim: alp-sdk pins and integrates upstream releases -- it does not fork or vendor library sources.

schema_version.description: Schema version this manifest is written against.  Currently 1.

name.description: Library token used in a project's `libraries:` array.  Lower-case, ASCII, hyphen-separated (e.g. `lvgl`, `cmsis-dsp`).  Must match the manifest filename (`<name>.yaml`) exactly.

description.description: Short customer-facing one-liner; surfaced in `alp doctor` and the portability matrix.

tier.description: Curation tier (ADR 0018).  A = curated: version-pinned, built in alp-sdk CI for at least one board per supported family, ships a teaching example, breakage blocks release.  B = recipe-only: wiring + compatibility metadata are maintained and emitted but the library is NOT built in alp-sdk CI (`alp doctor` labels it).

version.description: The pinned upstream version.  A human upstream release string (e.g. `9.5.0`) when the pinned tree exposes one, else the exact upstream revision the Zephyr west manifest / OE recipe pins.  Never a range -- the point is a single reproducible pin (ADR 0017).

license.description: SPDX licence identifier of the upstream library.  Validated against this permissive allowlist; a GPL-family or proprietary licence is rejected so a copyleft surprise cannot ride in through a `libraries:` selection (ADR 0018 non-goal).  Extending this allowlist is a deliberate human decision (legal review), not a metadata edit -- add the id here AND in metadata/libraries/README.md's allowlist rationale in the same change.  BSL-1.0 (Boost licence) and CC0-1.0 (public-domain-equivalent) were added by the #610 WS6-c maintainer legal-review decision, which covers two permissive curated-library manifests; that same review dropped two other curated tokens for carrying non-permissive copyleft/source-available licences instead of admitting those into this allowlist.

requires.description: Compatibility constraints resolved at emit time against the target SoM/core (ADR 0018).  Every constraint is optional; an omitted constraint means `no requirement`.  An unsatisfied constraint fails emit with the constraint named, exactly like schema validation.

capabilities.description: SoC/SoM capability keys the library needs (e.g. `gpu2d`, `cryptocell`).  Each key MUST name a real capability in the alp-sdk capability vocabulary -- `scripts/validate_metadata.py` cross-checks every key against metadata/schemas/soc-spec-v1.schema.json's `capabilities` properties and rejects an unknown key.  Resolved at emit time against the merged SoC+SoM `capabilities:`.

min_flash_kib.description: Minimum on-die/attached program store the target must have, in KiB.  Checked against the SoC spec's `soc_flash_mb` (x1024).

min_ram_kib.description: Minimum RAM the target must have, in KiB.  Checked against the SoC spec's `soc_ram_kb`.  For a GUI/heap library, derive this from the library's own configured heap floor and cite the source in the manifest.

core_class.description: Required application-core class: `m` (Cortex-M / Zephyr) or `a` (Cortex-A / Yocto).  Satisfied when the target SoC has at least one core of that class.  Only set this when upstream genuinely constrains it (e.g. a Zephyr Kconfig `depends on CPU_CORTEX_M`).

os.description: Operating systems the library can run under.  Satisfied when at least one of the project's cores runs one of these.  Usually left unset -- the presence/absence of an `integration.<os>` section already encodes per-OS support; use this only to reject a selection early with a clearer message.

integration.description: Per-OS build wiring the orchestrator emits.  Each OS section is optional; a library selected for a core whose OS has no section here fails emit (you asked for a library that cannot be wired on that OS).  At least one section is required.

zephyr.description: Zephyr wiring.  The library is pinned as a west module upstream (ADR 0017); this section records the module name and, when it exists, the Kconfig symbols that enable it.  Emitted into the slice's alp.conf fragment alongside the board/SoM-driven CONFIG lines.  At least one of `module`/`kconfig` must be present.  `kconfig` is NOT required, for two honest cases the schema must be able to express without inviting invention: (1) an enable-by-presence module -- it builds its objects (e.g. micro-ROS's `libmicroros`) whenever the west module is in the workspace, with no single `CONFIG_*=y` gate to transcribe; and (2) an ADR-curated module (e.g. the micro-ROS flagship) whose west pin is a documented prerequisite not yet in this checkout's west.yml, where transcribing a Kconfig would be an invention.  In both cases the section carries `module:` and the manifest header documents the prerequisite; the emitter renders the selection tag with no fabricated CONFIG line.  Never invent a Kconfig symbol to satisfy this section -- omit it and document why.

kconfig.description: Upstream Zephyr Kconfig assignments that enable the library and the components consumers reach (e.g. `CONFIG_LVGL=y`).  Transcribed from the pinned Zephyr module's Kconfig -- never invented.  Omitted for an enable-by-presence module or one whose west pin is still a prerequisite (see the section description).

module.description: West module name (as it appears in the Zephyr west manifest, e.g. `lvgl`), or null for a header-only/in-tree library with no separate module.  Documentation only -- west owns the actual pin.

west.description: Optional west project pin for a curated library whose upstream is not imported by Zephyr's own west manifest.  This is emitted by `alp_project.py --emit west-libraries` as a concrete project entry.  Use exact release tags or commit SHAs only; never a floating branch.

revision.description: Exact upstream release tag or commit SHA.  Floating branches such as `main` and `master` are rejected.

path.description: Workspace path where west should place the project.

yocto.description: Yocto wiring.  Emitted into the slice's local.conf fragment.  Only present when a real OE/meta-alp-sdk recipe exists -- never invented.

image_install.description: Recipe/package names appended to `IMAGE_INSTALL` (e.g. `lvgl` from the openembedded-layer).

recipe_include.description: Optional `.inc`/`require` line a consumer recipe must include to pull in the library's build glue.

baremetal.description: Baremetal / plain-CMake wiring.  Emitted into the slice's cmake args.  Only present when a real CMake package/FetchContent pin exists -- never invented.

cmake.description: CMake integration hint (a `find_package`/`FetchContent` pin the emitted args reference).
